faq.a1: Możesz konwertować między formatami WebP, PNG, JPG (JPEG) i ICO. Wszystkie konwersje odbywają się w przeglądarce przy użyciu Canvas API.
faq.q2: Czy moje obrazy są przesyłane na serwer?
faq.a2: Nie. Całe przetwarzanie obrazów odbywa się lokalnie w przeglądarce. Twoje obrazy nigdy nie opuszczają urządzenia.
faq.q3: Czy mogę konwertować wiele obrazów naraz?
faq.a3: Tak, możesz przeciągnąć i upuścić wiele obrazów, a wszystkie zostaną skonwertowane do wybranego formatu.
faq.q4: Can I use this tool offline?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page has loaded, the tool works completely offline. For the best experience, install PrivaDeck as a PWA from your browser.
faq.a5: There are no server-imposed limits. The maximum file size depends on your device's available memory and browser capabilities. Most modern devices handle files up to several hundred MB without issues.

intro.title: What is Image Format Converter?
intro.content:
Image Format Converter transforms images between popular formats including WebP, PNG, JPG, and ICO. Need to convert a WebP image to PNG for compatibility, or create an ICO favicon? This tool handles it instantly in your browser.

Supports batch conversion — drop multiple images and convert them all at once.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Format Converter
howToUse.content:
1. Upload one or more images by clicking the dropzone or dragging files onto the page
  2. Select the target format: WebP, PNG, JPG, or ICO
  3. Click Convert to process all images
  4. Download converted images individually or as a ZIP file

Supports input formats including WebP, PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, BMP, and SVG.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Multiple Formats: Convert between WebP, PNG, JPG, and ICO
  • Batch Conversion: Convert multiple images simultaneously
  • Quality Control: Adjust output quality for JPG and WebP
  • Favicon Support: Create ICO files for website favicons
  • Drag & Drop: Simply drag images onto the page to convert
  • Instant Processing: Uses Canvas API for fast, browser-based conversion

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Web Optimization: Convert images to WebP for smaller file sizes and faster websites
  • Compatibility: Convert WebP images to PNG or JPG for apps that don't support WebP
  • Favicon Creation: Generate ICO favicons from PNG or JPG images
  • Social Media: Convert images to formats required by different platforms
  • Print Preparation: Convert web images to print-compatible formats

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Conversion
privacy.content:
Images are converted entirely in your browser using the HTML5 Canvas API. No images are uploaded to any server.

  • Your images never leave your device
  • No server processing, no cloud storage, no third-party access
  • Works offline after the page loads
  • Verify: open DevTools (F12) → Network tab — zero upload requests

intro.title: What is Image Compressor?
intro.content:
Image Compressor reduces image file sizes while maintaining visual quality. Compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images with adjustable quality settings — see the file size reduction in real-time with a before/after comparison slider.

Ideal for optimizing website images, reducing email attachment sizes, or saving storage space.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Compressor
howToUse.content:
1. Upload images by clicking the dropzone or dragging files onto the page
  2. Adjust the quality slider to balance file size vs. visual quality
  3. Preview the compressed result with the before/after comparison slider
  4. Download compressed images individually or as a batch

The tool shows the original size, compressed size, and percentage saved for each image.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Adjustable Quality: Fine-tune compression level with a quality slider
  • Before/After Preview: Visual comparison slider to see quality differences
  • File Size Display: See original size, compressed size, and savings percentage
  • Batch Processing: Compress multiple images at once
  • Format Support: Compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images
  • Powered by browser-image-compression: Reliable compression using proven algorithms

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Web Performance: Reduce page load times by compressing website images
  • Email Attachments: Shrink images to fit within email size limits
  • Storage Savings: Reduce photo library size on your device
  • Social Media: Optimize images for faster uploads to social platforms
  • CMS Uploads: Meet file size limits for content management systems like WordPress

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Compression
privacy.content:
Images are compressed locally in your browser using the browser-image-compression library and Canvas API.

  • Your photos never leave your device — safe for personal and sensitive images
  • No cloud processing, no server uploads
  • Works offline after the page loads
  • Verify: open DevTools (F12) → Network tab — zero upload requests

intro.title: What is Image Crop?
intro.content:
Image Crop lets you interactively crop images to any size or aspect ratio. Use preset ratios for social media (1:1, 16:9, 4:3, 9:16) or draw a custom crop area by dragging on the image.

Perfect for preparing profile pictures, social media posts, thumbnails, or any image that needs precise framing.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Crop
howToUse.content:
1. Upload an image by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. Select a preset aspect ratio or choose Free for custom cropping
  3. Drag, resize, and position the crop area on the image
  4. Click Crop and download the cropped result

Zoom in/out to fine-tune the crop area. The output dimensions are shown in real-time.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Preset Aspect Ratios: 1:1 (square), 16:9 (widescreen), 4:3 (standard), 9:16 (portrait/stories)
  • Free Crop: Draw a custom crop area of any dimensions
  • Interactive UI: Drag, resize, and zoom the crop area visually
  • Real-Time Dimensions: See output width × height as you adjust
  • Powered by react-easy-crop: Smooth, touch-friendly cropping interface
  • High Quality Output: Crops at original image resolution

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Profile Pictures: Crop photos to square (1:1) for social media profiles
  • Social Media Posts: Crop to platform-specific ratios (16:9, 4:5, 9:16)
  • Thumbnails: Create video or article thumbnails at specific dimensions
  • Product Images: Crop product photos for e-commerce listings
  • Presentations: Crop images to fit slide layouts

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Cropping
privacy.content:

  • No server uploads or cloud processing

intro.title: What is Image Resize?
intro.content:
Image Resize changes image dimensions to exact pixel sizes or social media presets. Resize images for web, email, or print while maintaining aspect ratio — or set custom width and height values.

Includes presets for common sizes like Instagram posts, Facebook covers, Twitter headers, and more.

howToUse.title: How to Use Image Resize
howToUse.content:
1. Upload an image by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. Enter custom width and height in pixels, or select a social media preset
  3. Toggle Lock Aspect Ratio to prevent distortion
  4. Click Resize and download the resized image

When aspect ratio is locked, changing width automatically adjusts height and vice versa.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Custom Dimensions: Set exact width and height in pixels
  • Social Media Presets: Quick-select sizes for Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube
  • Aspect Ratio Lock: Prevent distortion by maintaining proportions
  • Batch Resize: Resize multiple images to the same dimensions
  • High-Quality Scaling: Canvas-based resizing with smooth interpolation
  • Real-Time Preview: See the resized image before downloading

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Social Media: Resize images for Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, or LinkedIn requirements
  • Website Images: Resize photos for consistent web page layouts
  • Email Marketing: Resize images to fit email template dimensions
  • Thumbnails: Create consistent-sized thumbnails for galleries or listings
  • Print: Resize images for specific print dimensions

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Resizing
privacy.content:

intro.title: What is Add Watermark?
intro.content:
Add Watermark overlays customizable text watermarks on your images. Protect your photos and artwork with your name, brand, or copyright notice — with full control over font, size, color, opacity, and position.

Process multiple images at once with the same watermark settings for batch protection.

howToUse.title: How to Use Add Watermark
howToUse.content:
1. Upload one or more images
  2. Enter your watermark text (e.g., your name, © notice, or brand)
  3. Customize appearance: font size, color, opacity, rotation, and position
  4. Preview the watermarked result and download

Adjust opacity to make the watermark subtle or prominent based on your needs.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Custom Text: Add any text as a watermark
  • Full Styling Control: Adjust font size, color, opacity, and rotation
  • Position Options: Place the watermark at center, corners, or custom positions
  • Batch Processing: Apply the same watermark to multiple images at once
  • Real-Time Preview: See the watermark on the image before downloading
  • Adjustable Opacity: From subtle to prominent watermark visibility

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Photography: Protect photos shared online with your name or logo
  • Art Portfolio: Mark artwork previews to prevent unauthorized use
  • Stock Images: Add watermarks to preview versions of stock photography
  • Business Documents: Mark documents as DRAFT, CONFIDENTIAL, or SAMPLE
  • Proofing: Send watermarked proofs to clients before final delivery

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Watermarking
privacy.content:
Watermarks are applied locally in your browser using the Canvas API.

  • Your images never leave your device — your original photos stay private
  • No server uploads or processing
  • Works offline after the page loads

intro.title: What is Remove EXIF Data?
intro.content:
Remove EXIF Data strips all metadata from your photos including GPS location, camera settings, timestamps, and device information. Protect your privacy before sharing photos online by removing data that reveals where and when a photo was taken.

Essential for privacy-conscious users sharing photos on social media, forums, or websites.

howToUse.title: How to Use Remove EXIF Data
howToUse.content:
1. Upload one or more photos by clicking the dropzone or dragging them onto the page
  2. Preview the EXIF metadata that will be removed (GPS, camera, date, etc.)
  3. Click Remove EXIF to strip all metadata
  4. Download the clean images with all metadata removed

The image quality and visual appearance remain completely unchanged — only the invisible metadata is removed.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Complete Metadata Removal: Strips GPS location, camera model, timestamps, software info, and all EXIF tags
  • Metadata Preview: See what metadata exists before removing it
  • Batch Processing: Strip metadata from multiple photos at once
  • Quality Preservation: Image pixels remain untouched — only metadata is removed
  • Multiple Format Support: Works with JPG, PNG, WebP, and TIFF images
  • Instant Processing: Metadata removal is nearly instantaneous

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Privacy Protection: Remove GPS coordinates before sharing photos online
  • Social Media: Strip personal metadata before posting to public platforms
  • Document Sharing: Remove camera and software info from professional images
  • Real Estate: Remove location data from property photos before listing
  • Journalism: Protect source locations by stripping photo metadata

privacy.title: Privacy-First EXIF Removal
privacy.content:
EXIF removal happens entirely in your browser — the irony of uploading photos to a server to remove privacy-revealing metadata would defeat the purpose.

  • Your photos never leave your device
  • No server uploads — metadata is stripped locally using Canvas API re-encoding
  • Works offline after the page loads

faq.a1: Odbicie poziome tworzy lustrzane odbicie z lewej na prawą, jak patrzenie w lustro. Odbicie pionowe tworzy odbicie z góry na dół.

intro.title: What is Flip Image?
intro.content:
Flip Image mirrors images horizontally (left to right) or vertically (top to bottom) in one click. Quick and simple — upload, flip, and download.

Useful for correcting mirrored selfies, creating symmetrical designs, or fixing image orientation.

howToUse.title: How to Use Flip Image
howToUse.content:
1. Upload an image by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. Click Flip Horizontal to mirror left/right, or Flip Vertical to mirror top/bottom
  3. Preview the result and download the flipped image

You can apply both flips to create a 180° rotation effect.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Horizontal Flip: Mirror image left to right
  • Vertical Flip: Mirror image top to bottom
  • Instant Processing: Flip happens immediately with Canvas API
  • Quality Preservation: No quality loss during flipping
  • Batch Support: Flip multiple images at once
  • Preview: See the flipped result before downloading

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Selfie Correction: Un-mirror selfie photos from front-facing cameras
  • Design Work: Create mirrored versions for symmetrical layouts
  • Print Preparation: Flip images for iron-on transfer printing
  • Photo Correction: Fix images that were scanned or photographed backwards
  • Art & Design: Check composition by viewing a mirrored version

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Flipping
privacy.content:
Images are flipped locally in your browser using the Canvas API.

  • Your images never leave your device
  • No server processing required
  • Works offline after the page loads

intro.title: What is Grayscale Image?
intro.content:
Grayscale Image converts color photos to black and white. Create classic monochrome versions of your images for artistic effect, print preparation, or document processing.

Upload any color image and get an instant grayscale conversion.

howToUse.title: How to Use Grayscale Image
howToUse.content:
1. Upload a color image by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. The grayscale conversion is applied automatically
  3. Preview the black-and-white result
  4. Download the grayscale image

Uses luminance-weighted conversion for natural-looking results (not simple desaturation).

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Luminance Conversion: Uses perceptual luminance weights for natural-looking grayscale
  • Instant Processing: Conversion happens immediately via Canvas API
  • Quality Preservation: Full resolution maintained
  • Batch Support: Convert multiple images at once
  • Multiple Formats: Works with JPG, PNG, WebP, and more
  • Preview: See the grayscale result before downloading

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Photography: Create artistic black-and-white versions of photos
  • Print: Prepare images for black-and-white printing
  • Documents: Convert images for use in grayscale documents or PDFs
  • Design: Create monochrome images for minimalist design projects
  • Thumbnails: Use grayscale for visual consistency in galleries

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Processing
privacy.content:
Grayscale conversion happens entirely in your browser using Canvas API pixel manipulation.

  • Your images never leave your device
  • No server processing required
  • Works offline after the page loads

faq.a1: Pikselizacja jest powszechnie używana do cenzurowania poufnych informacji na zrzutach ekranu, tworzenia retro efektów pixel-art lub anonimizacji twarzy na zdjęciach.
faq.a2: Tak. Użyj suwaka rozmiaru piksela, aby ustawić od 2px (subtelne) do 50px (silna pikselizacja).
faq.q3: Will my files be uploaded to a server?
faq.a3: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your device. You can verify this by opening DevTools (F12) and checking the Network tab — zero upload requests.
faq.q4: Can I use this tool offline?
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page has loaded, the tool works completely offline. For the best experience, install PrivaDeck as a PWA from your browser.
faq.a5: There are no server-imposed limits. The maximum file size depends on your device's available memory and browser capabilities. Most modern devices handle files up to several hundred MB without issues.

intro.title: What is Pixelate Image?
intro.content:
Pixelate Image applies a mosaic/pixelation effect to images with adjustable pixel size. Blur out faces, license plates, or sensitive information — or create retro pixel art effects.

Adjust the pixel block size to control the level of pixelation from subtle to heavy.

howToUse.title: How to Use Pixelate Image
howToUse.content:
1. Upload an image by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. Adjust the pixel size slider to control the pixelation intensity
  3. Preview the pixelated result in real-time
  4. Download the pixelated image

Higher pixel size values create larger blocks, making details more obscured.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Adjustable Pixel Size: Control the size of pixelation blocks with a slider
  • Real-Time Preview: See the effect change as you adjust the slider
  • Full Image Pixelation: Apply pixelation to the entire image
  • Quality Output: Clean pixel blocks rendered via Canvas API
  • Batch Support: Pixelate multiple images at once
  • Instant Processing: Effect is applied immediately

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Privacy Censoring: Pixelate faces, license plates, or personal info in photos
  • Screenshot Redaction: Hide sensitive data in screenshots before sharing
  • Pixel Art: Create retro pixel-art effects from photographs
  • Social Media: Anonymize people in photos before posting
  • Presentations: Obscure sensitive details in presentation images

privacy.title: Privacy-First Image Pixelation
privacy.content:
Pixelation is applied locally in your browser using Canvas API pixel manipulation.

  • Your images never leave your device — essential when pixelating sensitive content
  • No server processing or uploads
  • Works offline after the page loads

Circle Crop crops images into a perfect circle with a transparent background. Create round profile pictures, avatars, or circular design elements with a simple drag-and-zoom interface.

Output is a PNG with transparency — ready to use on any background.

intro.title: What is SVG to PNG?
intro.content:
SVG to PNG converts vector SVG graphics into raster PNG images at customizable scale (1x, 2x, 3x, 4x). Perfect for exporting SVG icons, logos, or illustrations as PNG at specific resolutions.

Preserve sharp edges and transparency while converting to a universally compatible image format.

howToUse.title: How to Use SVG to PNG
howToUse.content:
1. Upload an SVG file by clicking the dropzone or dragging it onto the page
  2. Select the output scale: 1x, 2x, 3x, or 4x
  3. Click Convert to generate the PNG
  4. Download the PNG image

Higher scales produce larger, higher-resolution PNGs suitable for retina displays.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• Multiple Scales: Export at 1x, 2x, 3x, or 4x resolution
  • Transparency Preserved: SVG transparency carries over to PNG
  • Sharp Rendering: High-quality rasterization of vector graphics
  • Batch Conversion: Convert multiple SVGs at once
  • Instant Processing: Fast conversion via Canvas API
  • Any SVG: Supports SVGs with embedded styles, gradients, and filters

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• App Icons: Export SVG icons as PNG at multiple sizes (1x, 2x, 3x)
  • Social Media: Convert SVG logos to PNG for platforms that don't support SVG
  • Email: Create PNG versions of SVG graphics for email templates
  • Documentation: Convert diagrams and illustrations from SVG to PNG
  • Print: Export high-resolution PNGs from SVG source files

privacy.title: Privacy-First SVG Conversion
privacy.content:

  • Your SVG files never leave your device
  • No server processing or uploads
  • Works offline after the page loads

intro.title: What is HEIC Converter?
intro.content:
HEIC Converter converts Apple HEIC/HEIF photos to widely compatible JPG or PNG formats. iPhones and iPads save photos in HEIC format by default — this tool lets you convert them for use anywhere without installing software.

Batch convert multiple HEIC files at once, all processed locally in your browser.

howToUse.title: How to Use HEIC Converter
howToUse.content:
1. Upload one or more HEIC/HEIF files from your iPhone, iPad, or Mac
  2. Select the output format: JPG or PNG
  3. Click Convert to process all files
  4. Download converted images individually or as a ZIP

For best compatibility, JPG is recommended. Choose PNG if you need transparency support.

features.title: Key Features
features.content:
• HEIC/HEIF Support: Convert Apple's default photo format
  • JPG & PNG Output: Choose your preferred output format
  • Batch Conversion: Convert multiple HEIC files simultaneously
  • Quality Preservation: High-quality conversion maintaining original colors
  • Powered by heic2any: Reliable conversion using the heic2any library
  • Drag & Drop: Simply drag HEIC files onto the page

useCases.title: Common Use Cases
useCases.content:
• Photo Sharing: Convert iPhone photos to JPG for sharing with Android or Windows users
  • Web Upload: Convert HEIC to JPG/PNG for websites that don't accept HEIC
  • Social Media: Prepare iPhone photos for platforms requiring JPG/PNG
  • Email: Convert to JPG for better email compatibility
  • Print: Convert to formats accepted by print services

privacy.content:
